articles.title: AAVE DAO VS. AAVE LABS - TOKEN HOLDERS LOOK TO RECLAIM CONTROL
articles.summary: An AAVE token holder has proposed a radical escalation in Aave governance, using a “poison pill” lawsuit to transfer Aave Labs' IP, trademarks, and even equity to the DAO, effectively turning the for-profit developer into a DAO subsidiary. The move follows backlash over a CoW Swap integration that redirected frontend swap fees from the DAO to a private address, reigniting long-standing tensions over who controls Aave's brand, revenue, and protocol direction.

articles.title: “DEFI WILL WIN”: AAVE CEO SAYS SEC ENDS YEARS-LONG PROBE
articles.summary: The SEC has formally concluded its four-year investigation into the Aave Protocol and does not plan to recommend enforcement action. The closure places Aave among a growing list of crypto firms cleared as the SEC shifts away from aggressive registration-focused enforcement.

articles.title: JAPAN'S SBI HOLDINGS TO LAUNCH YEN-PEGGED STABLECOIN
articles.summary: SBI Holdings partnered with Startale Group to launch a yen-denominated stablecoin in Q2 2026 with issuance handled by Shinsei Trust & Banking and circulation via licensed exchange SBI VC Trade, positioning it for global settlement and institutional adoption. The token will complement Startale's USDSC dollar stablecoin to power a 24/7 tokenized stock exchange and enable AI agent payments and tokenized asset distributions beyond everyday transactions. The project aligns with Japan's Financial Services Agency approving JPYC as the country's first yen stablecoin while supporting a joint initiative from Mizuho, MUFG, and SMBC. SBI separately partnered with Ripple to introduce RLUSD to the Japanese market in 2026.

articles.title: VISA OFFERS STABLECOIN SETTLEMENT FOR US BANKS USING CIRCLE'S USDC
articles.summary: Visa is opening its US network to stablecoin settlement, allowing banks to settle transactions using Circle's USDC on Solana. Cross River Bank and Lead Bank are among the first adopters. The move enables 24/7 settlement, including weekends and holidays, reframing stablecoins as a back-end treasury and settlement upgrade rather than a consumer payments product. With Visa's stablecoin volumes already running at multi-billion-dollar annualized levels, instant settlement is quickly becoming a competitive necessity for banks, not an experiment.

articles.title: AUTODELEVERAGING: $653 MILLION LOST TO A GREEDY HEURISTIC?
articles.summary: Hyperliquid and Binance's Queue ADL algorithm liquidated $653 million more trader profit than necessary to cover bad debt in a liquidation cascade on October 10. The greedy heuristic from 2015 targeted whale market makers 40+ times over 12 minutes, while risk-agnostic Pro-Rata spread losses equally without accounting for leverage differences. New research proposes Risk-Aware Pro-Rata mechanisms to reweight haircuts based on trader leverage to balance the ADL Trilemma, where no strategy simultaneously optimizes solvency, fairness, and revenue. Convex optimization shows that prior-independent strategies outperform Queue by orders of magnitude.

articles.title: THE GOLDEN AGE OF CRYPTO IS OVER
articles.summary: Crypto mirrors poker's 2003-2014 golden age decline as simple strategies stop working. Crypto is currently at poker's 2012 inflection point, where opportunities exist but require discipline, work ethic, and edge-finding through whitepapers, thesis development, and trading logs versus easy 10x NFT mints and abundant airdrops. Retail already cycled through multiple burn-and-leave episodes rather than waiting to arrive, now entering via small-percentage Bitcoin ETF DCA allocations that support adoption without fueling speculative golden eras, while competition is intensifying, requiring constant studying and latest tool utilization, like poker's mid-2010s solver adoption. Unlike poker's zero-sum game with bleak long-term outlook, limited by player count, crypto adds real technological value with inevitable tenfold adoption growth, practical use cases, and constructive regulation, making the long-term view bright despite the easy-money golden age ending.

articles.title: MOST USED CHAIN BASED ON WHAT?
articles.summary: Solana's TVL market share surged from 0.7% to 9.5% since 2023, while Ethereum L2s like Optimism and Arbitrum stagnated or declined. Solana's fee capture has exploded 123x from 0.17% to 22.39%, now matching Ethereum's 40% monthly share. The network has dominated 82% of blockchain transactions since January 2023 and doubled Ethereum's DEX aggregation volume while user share climbed from 6% to 28% versus Ethereum's 70% decline from 20% to 6%. While Ethereum remains the most valuable decentralized social platform for monetary applications, numerical literacy demands acknowledging that Solana leads on user count, transaction count, volume, DEX aggregation, TVL growth, and fees.

articles.title: CRYPTO CARDS DON'T HAVE ANY FUTURE
articles.summary: Crypto cards add abstraction layers, strengthening Visa and Mastercard gatekeepers rather than replacing them, as most function as liquidity bridges, selling crypto for fiat on each transaction, with infrastructure providers like Rain enabling companies to launch branded cards without significant engineering. Non-custodial cards lock users into specific ecosystems like MetaMask on Linea or Plasma Card, creating Apple-style habit formation for natural liquidity attraction rather than mining campaigns, though they lack privacy since KYC links crypto addresses to real identities. EtherFi stands out as the only viable long-term model by collateralizing crypto for fiat loans, avoiding taxable selling events on each purchase while generating yield, positioning it as the true DeFi-TradFi intersection versus competitors capitalizing on narratives without vertical innovation.

articles.title: ETH HAS MORE LONG-TERM UPSIDE THAN BTC
articles.summary: While Bitcoin excels as digital gold, Ethereum captures significantly more long-term upside by serving as the programmable settlement layer for stablecoins, RWAs, DeFi, NFTs, and rollups.

articles.title: ETHEREUM DA FEES RISE AFTER FUSAKA AS BLOB PRICING TIGHTENS
articles.summary: Ethereum's Data Availability fees are starting to reflect a higher blob floor price following the Fusaka upgrade, with visible spikes in DA revenue.

articles.title: A 5-YEAR REALITY CHECK, LEBANON AND CRYPTO
articles.summary: Bitcoin is up ~4.2x over the past five years, while the Lebanese pound has lost over 98% of its value.

articles.title: FDIC MOVES TO OPERATIONALIZE BANK-ISSUED STABLECOINS
articles.summary: The FDIC approved a proposed rule to implement application procedures under the GENIUS Act, allowing FDIC-supervised banks to issue payment stablecoins through approved subsidiaries.
